my_blog
  • À propos de moi

olympicsWeather

package R
Author

Paul Nestour

Published

March 16, 2024

Librairie olympicsWeather

La librairie olympicsWeather fournit des fonctionnalités pour accéder aux données de prévisions météorologiques en fonction des coordonnées géographiques ou des adresses. Avant d’utiliser la librairie, vous devez l’installer. Voici les étapes pour installer la librairie en utilisant le package remotes depuis GitHub.

# Installer le package remotes s'il n'est pas déjà installé
install.packages("remotes")
The following package(s) will be installed:
- remotes [2.4.2.1]
These packages will be installed into "~/Documents/my_blog/renv/library/R-4.3/x86_64-apple-darwin20".

# Installing packages --------------------------------------------------------
- Installing remotes ...                        OK [linked from cache]
Successfully installed 1 package in 13 milliseconds.
# Charger le package remotes
library(remotes)

# Installer le package olympicsWeather depuis GitHub
remotes::install_github("paulnest/olympicsWeather")
Skipping install of 'olympicsWeather' from a github remote, the SHA1 (9f365a42) has not changed since last install.
  Use `force = TRUE` to force installation
# Charger la librairie
library(olympicsWeather)

# Voici la liste des packages nécessaires au fonctionnement de olympicsWeather (à installer si le chargement du package seul ne le fait pas)
library(dplyr)

Attaching package: 'dplyr'
The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag
The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(httr)
library(httr2)
library(jsonlite)
library(tibble)
library(tidygeocoder)
library(tidyverse)
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ readr     2.1.5
✔ ggplot2   3.5.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
✔ purrr     1.0.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter()  masks stats::filter()
✖ purrr::flatten() masks jsonlite::flatten()
✖ dplyr::lag()     masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(opencage)

Quelques exemples de prévisions Météo

Quelles sont les prévisions météo à 7 jours pour le stade de France ?

get_forecast("Stade de France")
Passing 1 address to the Nominatim single address geocoder
Query completed in: 1 seconds
# A tibble: 168 × 5
   date_heure       temperature_celsius temperature_ressenti…¹ precipation_proba
   <chr>                          <dbl>                  <dbl>             <int>
 1 2024-03-17T00:00                11.2                    9.9                 0
 2 2024-03-17T01:00                10.8                    9.7                 1
 3 2024-03-17T02:00                10.6                    9.4                 2
 4 2024-03-17T03:00                10.5                    9                   3
 5 2024-03-17T04:00                10.5                    8.9                14
 6 2024-03-17T05:00                10.4                    8.8                24
 7 2024-03-17T06:00                10.5                    9.2                35
 8 2024-03-17T07:00                10.6                    9.4                34
 9 2024-03-17T08:00                10.9                    9.5                33
10 2024-03-17T09:00                11.6                    9.8                32
# ℹ 158 more rows
# ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​temperature_ressentie_celsius
# ℹ 1 more variable: precipitation <dbl>

Pour Tahiti ?

get_forecast("Tahiti")
Passing 1 address to the Nominatim single address geocoder
Query completed in: 1 seconds
# A tibble: 168 × 5
   date_heure       temperature_celsius temperature_ressenti…¹ precipation_proba
   <chr>                          <dbl>                  <dbl>             <int>
 1 2024-03-17T00:00                17.6                   21                  35
 2 2024-03-17T01:00                17.6                   19.8                25
 3 2024-03-17T02:00                17.6                   19                  16
 4 2024-03-17T03:00                17.6                   19.2                 6
 5 2024-03-17T04:00                17.6                   19.2                 7
 6 2024-03-17T05:00                17.7                   19.2                 9
 7 2024-03-17T06:00                17.6                   19                  10
 8 2024-03-17T07:00                17.6                   18.9                22
 9 2024-03-17T08:00                17.5                   18.9                33
10 2024-03-17T09:00                17.3                   18.8                45
# ℹ 158 more rows
# ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​temperature_ressentie_celsius
# ℹ 1 more variable: precipitation <dbl>

Pour les coordonnées gps 43.276703, 5.334791 ?

get_forecast(c(43.276703, 5.334791))
# A tibble: 168 × 5
   date_heure       temperature_celsius temperature_ressenti…¹ precipation_proba
   <chr>                          <dbl>                  <dbl>             <int>
 1 2024-03-17T00:00                14.9                   11.6                 0
 2 2024-03-17T01:00                13.9                    9.5                 0
 3 2024-03-17T02:00                14.9                   13.3                 0
 4 2024-03-17T03:00                11.5                   10.5                 0
 5 2024-03-17T04:00                11.4                   10.3                 0
 6 2024-03-17T05:00                11.8                   10.6                 0
 7 2024-03-17T06:00                11.8                   10.3                 0
 8 2024-03-17T07:00                11.9                   10.8                 0
 9 2024-03-17T08:00                11.7                   11.1                 0
10 2024-03-17T09:00                12.6                   12                   0
# ℹ 158 more rows
# ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​temperature_ressentie_celsius
# ℹ 1 more variable: precipitation <dbl>
